checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated text. All headings and list items present in the output have a direct basis in the source document. No introductions, conclusions, or page markers have been invented.

No fabricated URLs, phone numbers, or addresses were found. The document mentions meeting details and websites; while some URLs are missing (see C6), no incorrect or invented contact information is present.

All primary sections of the agenda (Welcome, Framework, Working Groups, Regular Status Updates, Other Business, Action Item Review, Adjourn) are represented in the output. While significant content within these sections is missing, the sections themselves are not omitted.
The source document does not contain any formal tables. Data is presented in lists and paragraphs.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The conversion has failed to capture all nested list items (sub-bullets) throughout the document. This results in the loss of crucial details for nearly every major section. Specifically, sub-points under Elevation, Administrative/Governmental Units, NC Board on Geographic Names, Metadata Committee, and Other Business are entirely absent. This constitutes a loss of more than 25% of the text content within those sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: Multiple nested list items: 'o Updates on Contours...', 'o County Boundaries...', 'o Municipal Boundaries...', 'o SMAC Discussion and Approval', '▪ Simone Mountain', '▪ Stiles Mountain', 'o Update on refresh of documents/tools...', 'o Adjustment to reflect the correct statutes...'
checks.b_evidence_output: The output lists only the parent items. For example, the 'items' array under 'Elevation - John Lay' has 'nested_list': null.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections: Framework+ Datasets, Working Groups, Other Business

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are several instances of minor text omissions that alter the completeness of information. The top header line containing the website URLs is missing. The word 'minutes' is missing from the first list item. The link text 'Join meeting' is missing from the Web Conference paragraph.
checks.b_evidence_source:
1. 'SMAC Web Site – http://it.nc.gov/gicc | NC OneMap Web Site - www.nconemap.gov'
2. 'Approval of October 14, 2020 minutes'
3. 'Web Conference: Join meeting'
checks.b_evidence_output:

2. 'Approval of October 14, 2020 '
3. '**Web Conference:** '

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text or sections. Each element from the source PDF appears exactly once in the structured JSON, maintaining the one-to-one relationship with the source content.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows best practices for accessibility. The document title is an H1, major agenda items are H2, and the sidebar ('2021 Meetings') is correctly identified as an H3 within the logical flow of the 'Working Groups' section. No levels are skipped.
The source document does not contain any tables. The textual content, including the sidebar data, has been rendered as headings, paragraphs, or lists, which is appropriate for the source layout.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical flow of the agenda. The sidebar ('2021 Meetings') has been integrated into the sequence following the 'Working Groups' section it was visually adjacent to. This reordering is a standard optimization for screen readers and does not disrupt the logic of the document.

The conversion failed to capture any nested list items (sub-bullets) present in the source PDF. For example, under 'Elevation', the sub-bullet 'Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”' is missing. Similarly, the multi-level nesting under 'NC Board on Geographic Names' (SMAC Discussion and Approval > Simone/Stiles Mountain) is completely absent, with 'nested_list' values set to null. Additionally, the list item 'Approval of October 14, 2020 minutes' is truncated, missing the word 'minutes'.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Elevation – John Lay
• Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”

NC Board on Geographic Names - Cam McNutt
• SMAC Discussion and Approval
▪ Simone Mountain
▪ Stiles Mountain

checks.a_analysis: Several active links in the source PDF are missing from the output. The link 'Join meeting' in the header is entirely omitted, leaving the 'Web Conference:' label empty. The link on the word 'minutes' in the first list item is missing, and the word itself is truncated. Additionally, the URLs in the footer were not captured in the output.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Web Conference: Join meeting

Approval of October 14, 2020 minutes
checks.b_evidence_location: Header paragraph (reading_order 3) and first list item (reading_order 6)

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output fails to maintain the visual organization of the source document in two major ways. First, the hierarchical structure of the nested lists is lost; the source PDF uses sub-bullets and sub-sub-bullets (e.g., under 'Elevation', 'Administrative/Governmental Units', 'NC Board on Geographic Names', and 'Other Business') to organize information. In the HTML rendering, these sub-levels are entirely missing, resulting in a flat and incomplete list. Second, the visual flow of the 'Framework+ Datasets' section is broken: the first list item ('Orthoimagery') is positioned next to the sidebar TOC, while the subsequent seven items are orphaned below the sidebar container, creating a significant visual gap and disconnecting the items from their parent heading. These issues collectively represent a major failure in preserving the document's visual organization and intended flow.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 1 shows 'Elevation - John Lay' with a nested sub-bullet 'Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”'; 'Administrative/Governmental Units' has two nested sub-bullets for County and Municipal Boundaries. The 'Framework+ Datasets' list is a contiguous block of 8 bullets.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows 'Elevation - John Lay' as a single bullet with no following content. The 'Framework+ Datasets' section heading is followed by only one bullet ('Orthoimagery'), with the rest of the list appearing orphaned below the sidebar block.
checks.b_evidence_location: Framework+ Datasets and Working Groups sections

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any formal data tables. The list of meeting dates is rendered as text and does not require table structures.
The source PDF is entirely text-based and does not contain any meaningful images, logos, or diagrams that would need to be rendered in the HTML.
There is no visual duplication of body content. While headers appear in both the sidebar table of contents and the main body, this is an intentional and expected design for accessible navigation.
